_comment: Hand-derived (implementation-plan.md fixtures rule, WP-13). Nutrition Log is a Sample (S) record type per base-knowledge.md §3 -- like weight.json/height.json, not a nested-payload Session (Se) like sleep.json/exercise -- so this fixture assumes one GoogleDataPoint represents one whole meal/log entry, with all four macro fields flat in `value` (no sessionPayload). base-knowledge.md documents neither Google's exact field names for this type nor its wire shape beyond the `<data_type>.<field>` nesting convention (§2) -- field names `nutrition_log.energy_kcal`/`.protein_g`/`.carbs_g`/`.fat_g` are invented, chosen for consistency with this fixture set's existing short, unit-suffixed naming style (active-energy-burned.json's `kcal`, blood-glucose-mgdl.json's `mg_per_dl`). This is the full-macro variant (all four present) -- see nutrition-log-partial.json for WP-13's required 'meal missing macros' scenario. `dataPointId` (`nutrition-0001`) is this design's meal-grouping key (WP-13: 'meal grouping key = Google log entry ID') -- see SyncKit's MappedNutritionCorrelation doc comment for the full one-point-per-meal assumption and its flagged need for reconciliation against a real payload. Mirrored in SyncKit's TypeMapperFixtures.nutritionLogPoint().
